mnemonics: GIFT: A Group must have an Identity element and each element must have an Inverse, defined over a closed Function (operation), and must be Associative. Think of the group as a GIFT that comes with these four necessary parts.
keyPoints: A group is a set equipped with a binary operation that is closed, associative, has an identity element, and every element has an inverse.
Abelian (or commutative) groups are a special type of group where the operation is commutative (a * b = b * a).
The order of a group is the number of elements in its set. The order of an element is the smallest positive integer n such that gⁿ = e (the identity).
commonMistakes: Assuming all operations are commutative. Group theory does not require commutativity; that is a special property of Abelian groups.
Forgetting to check for closure. A set must be closed under the defined operation to be considered a group.
Confusing the identity element (e.g., 0 for addition, 1 for multiplication) and inverses (e.g., -a for addition, 1/a for multiplication) when working with common number sets.
